# Advanced Drainage Systems (WMS)

## Company Overview
Advanced Drainage Systems (ADS) is the largest manufacturer of high-performance thermoplastic corrugated pipe and related water management products in North America. Headquartered in Hilliard, Ohio, the company provides a comprehensive suite of stormwater and onsite wastewater management solutions for residential, non-residential, agricultural, and infrastructure end markets.

ADS has been transforming from a pipe manufacturing company into a full-scale water management solutions provider. This evolution was dramatically accelerated by the $1.0B acquisition of National Diversified Sales (NDS) in early 2026, which added landscape irrigation, residential water management, and access box products to the portfolio. Combined with its existing stormwater drainage, retention/detention, and filtration systems, ADS now offers one of the broadest water management product portfolios in North America.

The company benefits from several secular tailwinds: aging water infrastructure, increasingly stringent stormwater regulations, the shift from concrete to thermoplastic pipe (lighter, cheaper, easier to install), and growing emphasis on sustainable water management in both residential and commercial construction.

## Key Financial Data

ADS operates on a fiscal year ending March 31. Current reporting is for Fiscal Year 2026.

| Metric | Q3 FY2026 (Dec 2025) | Q3 FY2025 | Change |
|--------|----------------------|-----------|--------|
| Net Sales | $693.4M | $690.6M | +0.4% |
| Net Income | $94.0M | $82.3M | +14.3% |
| EPS (diluted) | $1.19 | $1.04 | +14.4% |

| Metric | Q2 FY2026 (Sep 2025) | Q2 FY2025 | Change |
|--------|----------------------|-----------|--------|
| Net Sales | $850.4M | $782.6M | +8.7% |
| Net Income | $156.5M | $131.2M | +19.3% |
| Adjusted EBITDA | $287.5M | $245.5M | +17.1% |
| Adj. EBITDA Margin | 33.8% | 31.4% | +240 bps |

## Recent Earnings

### Q3 Fiscal 2026 (Quarter Ended December 31, 2025)
Net sales increased modestly by 0.4% to $693.4M (seasonal Q3 slowdown). Despite minimal revenue growth, net income increased 14.3% to $94.0M driven by margin expansion and cost management. The company also increased its stock repurchase authorization, signaling confidence in the outlook.

### FY2026 YTD Performance
Through the first nine months of FY2026, ADS has demonstrated consistent margin improvement with adjusted EBITDA margins exceeding 30%, driven by favorable pricing, recycled material cost advantages, and operational leverage.

## M&A Activity (2025-2026)

| Date | Target | Value | Revenue | Description |
|------|--------|-------|---------|-------------|
| Feb 2026 | National Diversified Sales (NDS) | ~$1.0B ($875M adj. for tax benefits) | $313M TTM | Water management business of Norma Group SE; landscape irrigation, residential water management |

**NDS Acquisition Details:**
- Approximately 90% of NDS sales are in the U.S.
- Expected to deliver over $25M in annual cost synergies within three years
- Provides entry into the $1.5B landscape irrigation segment
- Expected to be accretive to adjusted EPS in the first year
- Transforms ADS into a comprehensive water management solutions provider

## Strategic Initiatives

- **Water Management Platform:** Evolving from pipe manufacturer to full-scale water management solutions provider spanning stormwater, onsite wastewater, and now landscape irrigation.
- **NDS Integration:** Near-term priority to capture cost synergies and cross-sell opportunities from the NDS acquisition.
- **Recycled Materials Advantage:** ADS uses recycled HDPE (high-density polyethylene) as a primary raw material, providing both cost and sustainability advantages over virgin material competitors.
- **Infrastructure Spending:** Positioned to benefit from federal infrastructure investment in water management systems.
- **Market Share Gains:** Continuing to take share from concrete and metal pipe alternatives through product performance and installation cost advantages.

## Tariff & Trade Exposure

ADS has relatively low direct tariff exposure given its predominantly North American manufacturing footprint and domestic raw material sourcing (recycled HDPE from U.S. sources). However, tariffs on imported resin, equipment, and specialty components could modestly increase costs. Rising material costs (particularly recycled polypropylene and HDPE) represent a more meaningful input cost risk than tariffs.

## 2026 Outlook & Guidance

### Fiscal Year 2026 (Ending March 31, 2026)

| Metric | Guidance Range |
|--------|---------------|
| Net Sales | $2.990B - $3.040B |
| Adjusted EBITDA | $930M - $960M |
| Capital Expenditures | ~$250M |

The guidance reflects organic growth plus the contribution from the NDS acquisition. Key growth drivers include continued stormwater management demand, NDS integration benefits, and market share gains from plastic-to-concrete conversion.

## News Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| 2026-02-05 | Q3 FY2026 results: Revenue +0.4%, net income +14.3%; share repurchase authorization increased |
| 2026-02-02 | Completed acquisition of National Diversified Sales (NDS) from Norma Group |
| 2025-11-06 | Q2 FY2026 results: Revenue +8.7%, Adj. EBITDA margin 33.8% |
| 2025-09-22 | Announced definitive agreement to acquire NDS for ~$1.0B |

## Related Articles
- [Bain M&A Report](../../raw/articles/2026-04-06-bain-building-products-ma-report.md)

companies.content:
# AGC Inc. (5201.T)

## Company Overview

AGC Inc. (formerly Asahi Glass Co.) is a Japan-based multinational corporation and one of the world's largest glass manufacturers. Founded in 1907 and headquartered in Tokyo, AGC operates across four core business segments: Architectural Glass, Automotive Glass, Electronics, and Chemicals. The company produces flat glass, automotive glass, display glass, specialty chemicals, ceramics, and advanced electronic materials. AGC serves the construction, automotive, electronics, and chemical industries globally, with operations spanning Asia, Europe, and the Americas.

AGC holds leading global market positions in architectural glass, automotive glass, and EUV photomask blanks for semiconductor manufacturing. The company is a critical supplier in the semiconductor supply chain through its EUV mask blank technology. With annual revenue exceeding JPY 2 trillion (approximately $13-14 billion), AGC is the largest glass company in Asia and among the top three globally alongside Saint-Gobain and NSG/Pilkington.

The company has been undergoing a strategic portfolio transformation under its medium-term management plan "AGC plus-2026," emphasizing "ambidextrous management" to balance core business optimization with growth investments in high-value segments like electronics and life sciences.

## Key Financial Data

| Metric | FY2025 | FY2024 | Change |
|--------|--------|--------|--------|
| Net Sales | JPY 2,058.8B | JPY 2,067.6B | -0.4% |
| Operating Profit | JPY 127.5B | JPY 125.9B | +1.3% |
| ROE | 4.7% | ~4.5% | +0.2pp |
| Q4 2025 Revenue | JPY 546.7B | - | +0.14% vs forecast |
| Q3 2025 Revenue | JPY 516.7B | - | +3.26% vs forecast |

## Recent Earnings

**FY2025 Full Year (ended December 2025):**
- Net sales of JPY 2,058.8 billion, declining JPY 8.8 billion year-over-year
- Operating profit improved to JPY 127.5 billion (+JPY 1.6B YoY)
- ROE of 4.7%
- Q4 2025 revenue of JPY 546.69 billion (0.14% above forecast)
- Q3 2025 was a standout quarter with operating profit surpassing JPY 40 billion for the first time in three years
- CEO Yoshinori Hirai indicated the company has "hit the bottom" and expects recovery ahead

**Segment Highlights:**
- Electronics segment benefited from strong EUV mask blank demand (achieved JPY 40B in sales in 2024, one year ahead of target)
- Chemicals segment expected to see growth from 2026
- Architectural glass remained under pressure from weak construction markets

## M&A Activity (2025-2026)

| Date | Transaction | Details |
|------|------------|---------|
| Nov 2025 | Divestiture: Al Obeikan AGC Glass JV | AGC transferred all shares held via AGC France Holding SAS to Obeikan Glass Company |

AGC's M&A strategy under the plus-2026 plan focuses on portfolio optimization rather than aggressive acquisitions. The company is prioritizing structural reforms in low-ROCE businesses and selective investment in high-growth areas. Large-scale capacity expansion investments were completed in 2025, with new investments to be carefully selected going forward.

## Strategic Initiatives

- **AGC plus-2026 Medium-Term Plan:** "Ambidextrous management" strategy targeting a business portfolio resilient to market fluctuations with high asset efficiency, growth potential, and carbon efficiency
- **EUV Photomask Blanks:** Key growth driver in semiconductor supply chain; sales expected to increase again from 2026 onward after hitting JPY 40B milestone ahead of schedule
- **Thailand Facility Expansion:** New expanded facility expected to start operations in 2025 and contribute to profits from 2026
- **Structural Reforms:** Accelerating reforms in businesses with low ROCE while maintaining investment in innovation
- **Carbon Efficiency:** Targeting improved carbon efficiency across the portfolio as part of sustainability strategy
- **Portfolio Transformation:** Active rotation between core and strategic businesses to optimize returns

## Tariff & Trade Exposure

AGC's global manufacturing footprint mitigates some tariff risk, but the company faces exposure through:
- **Glass imports:** Potential impact from trade tensions between Japan, China, and the US on glass products
- **Chemicals segment:** Cross-border chemical trade subject to various tariff regimes
- **Electronics components:** Semiconductor supply chain subject to export controls and trade restrictions between US, Japan, and China
- **European operations:** AGC Glass Europe operations face energy cost pressures and EU carbon border adjustment mechanism (CBAM) considerations
- **Mitigation:** Localized manufacturing in key markets (Japan, Europe, Southeast Asia) provides natural hedging against trade barriers

## 2026 Outlook & Guidance

**Official FY2026 Guidance:**
- Net sales forecast: JPY 2.2 trillion (+JPY 141.2B, ~7% growth)
- Operating profit forecast: JPY 150 billion (+JPY 22.5B, ~18% growth)
- ROE target: 5.0% in FY2026, with 8%+ targeted by FY2027

**Key Catalysts:**
- Recovery in glass markets as construction activity improves
- EUV mask blank sales resuming growth trajectory
- Thailand facility contributing to profits
- Chemicals segment growth expected from 2026
- Completion of structural reforms improving margins

**Risks:**
- Weak global construction markets
- Semiconductor demand cyclicality
- Energy cost volatility in Europe
- Currency fluctuations (JPY/USD, JPY/EUR)

## News Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| Feb 2026 | FY2025 full-year results released; FY2026 guidance issued |
| Nov 2025 | Completed divestiture of Al Obeikan AGC Glass joint venture shares |
| Nov 2025 | Q3 2025 results exceeded expectations; operating profit surpassed JPY 40B for first time in 3 years |
| Aug 2025 | Company overview and strategic update published |
| Jan 2025 | Exhibited at CES 2025, showcasing cutting-edge materials and solutions |

## Related Articles

- Search knowledge base for articles mentioning "AGC" or "Asahi Glass"
- Cross-reference with semiconductor industry coverage for EUV mask blank developments

# ArcelorMittal (MT)

## Company Overview

ArcelorMittal S.A. is the world's largest steel and mining company, headquartered in Luxembourg City, Luxembourg. The company was formed in 2006 through the merger of Arcelor and Mittal Steel. ArcelorMittal operates in over 60 countries, producing a comprehensive range of steel products including flat carbon steel, long carbon steel, stainless steel, and tubular products. The company also has significant iron ore and coal mining operations, providing partial raw material self-sufficiency.

ArcelorMittal produced 55.6 million metric tonnes of crude steel and 48.8 million tonnes of iron ore in 2025. The company operates through multiple segments including NAFTA (North America), Europe, Brazil, ACIS (Africa and CIS), and Mining. ArcelorMittal is a key supplier to the automotive, construction, appliance, packaging, and energy industries globally.

The company has been executing a major strategic growth plan centered on high-return capital projects and targeted M&A. Key initiatives include the full acquisition of the Calvert, Alabama steel facility from Nippon Steel, a $1.2 billion NOES (non-grain-oriented electrical steel) facility investment at Calvert, and expansion projects in Brazil and Liberia. These growth projects are expected to increase future EBITDA potential by $2.1 billion.

## Key Financial Data

| Metric | FY2025 | FY2024 | Change |
|--------|--------|--------|--------|
| Revenue | $61.35B | $62.44B | -1.7% |
| Earnings | $3.15B | ~$1.34B | +135.4% |
| Crude Steel Production | 55.6Mt | - | - |
| Iron Ore Production | 48.8Mt | - | - |
| Q4 2025 EPS | $0.86 | - | Beat $0.62 est. by 38.7% |
| Q4 2025 Revenue | $14.97B | - | Missed $15.58B est. |

## Recent Earnings

**FY2025 Full Year Results:**
- Revenue of $61.35 billion, down 1.74% from $62.44 billion in 2024
- Earnings of $3.15 billion, up 135.4% year-over-year (boosted by $1.5B Calvert gain)
- Crude steel production of 55.6 million metric tonnes
- Iron ore production of 48.8 million tonnes

**Q4 2025 Results:**
- EPS of $0.86, significantly outperforming analyst forecast of $0.62 (+38.7%)
- Revenue of $14.97 billion, slightly below expectations of $15.58 billion
- Strategic growth projects driving earnings beat despite revenue softness

**Exceptional Items:**
- Approximately $1.5 billion exceptional gain recorded in Q2 2025 from the Calvert acquisition completion

## M&A Activity (2025-2026)

| Date | Transaction | Value | Details |
|------|------------|-------|---------|
| Jun 2025 | Acquisition: Nippon Steel's 50% stake in AM/NS Calvert | ~$0.9B (cash + loans) | Full ownership of Calvert facility; $1 for shares + ~$0.9B in cash/loans; $1.5B exceptional gain |
| Mar 2025 | Acquisition: Tuper (Brazil) | TBD | Steel solutions for automotive, construction, and energy sectors |
| Feb 2025 | Investment: NOES facility at Calvert | $1.2B | Advanced non-grain-oriented electrical steel; 150,000 Mt annual capacity |

**Strategic Growth Projects (EBITDA Impact):**
- Total expected EBITDA increase from strategic projects and M&A: $2.1 billion
- 2025 targeted contribution: $0.7 billion
- 2026 targeted contribution: $0.8 billion
- Key projects: Serra Azul and Barra Mansa (Brazil), Liberia expansion, Calvert EAF ramp-up, NOES facility

## Strategic Initiatives

- **Calvert Transformation:** Full ownership and $1.2B NOES investment; 1.5Mtpa EAF ramping toward full utilization
- **Decarbonization:** Investing in green steel technologies and EAF conversion
- **Growth Projects:** Serra Azul (Brazil iron ore), Barra Mansa (Brazil steel), Liberia expansion
- **Vertical Integration:** Mining operations providing partial raw material self-sufficiency
- **Capital Returns:** Active share buyback program alongside growth investments
- **NOES for EV/Renewables:** 150,000 Mt annual capacity targeting automotive, mobility, and renewable energy markets

## Tariff & Trade Exposure

- **Section 232 Beneficiary (US operations):** Calvert facility benefits from 50% steel tariffs protecting domestic production
- **European Exposure:** EU operations face competition from cheap imports; EU has safeguard measures in place
- **Canadian Retaliation:** Canada imposing 25% retaliatory tariffs on US steel and aluminum exports
- **Global Trade Complexity:** Operations in 60+ countries create complex multi-jurisdictional tariff exposure
- **Raw Materials:** Iron ore and coal trade subject to various export taxes and trade restrictions
- **Net Assessment:** US operations are net beneficiaries of Section 232; international operations face mixed tariff environment

## 2026 Outlook & Guidance

**Company Outlook:**
- Strategic growth projects expected to contribute $0.8 billion incremental EBITDA in 2026
- Calvert EAF ramping toward full 1.5Mtpa utilization
- NOES facility under construction at Calvert
- Brazil and Liberia expansions progressing

**Key Catalysts:**
- Full ramp-up of Calvert EAF to 1.5Mtpa
- Strategic project EBITDA contributions ($0.8B targeted in 2026)
- Steel price recovery from current levels
- Infrastructure spending in US and developing markets
- NOES demand from EV and renewables transition

**Key Risks:**
- Global steel overcapacity, particularly from China
- Economic slowdown reducing steel demand
- Trade policy volatility and retaliatory tariffs
- Iron ore price weakness
- Currency fluctuations

**Financial Calendar:**
- 2026 earnings release dates announced; May 5 AGM scheduled

## News Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| Feb 2026 | Q4/FY2025 results released; EPS significantly beat estimates |
| Jun 2025 | Completed acquisition of Nippon Steel's 50% Calvert stake; recorded $1.5B gain |
| Mar 2025 | Completed acquisition of Tuper in Brazil |
| Feb 2025 | Announced $1.2B NOES facility investment at Calvert |
| 2025 | Section 232 steel tariffs increased to 50% |
| 2025 | Produced 55.6Mt of crude steel and 48.8Mt of iron ore |

## Related Articles

- Search knowledge base for articles mentioning "ArcelorMittal" or "Calvert"
- Cross-reference with Section 232 steel tariff coverage and global steel market articles

# ASSA ABLOY (ASSA-B.ST)

## Company Overview
ASSA ABLOY is the global leader in access solutions, headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden. The company manufactures and sells locks, doors, access control systems, and related security products through four divisions: Americas, EMEIA, Asia Pacific, and Global Technologies. With annual revenue of approximately SEK 152B (~$14.5B USD), ASSA ABLOY holds the number one or two market position in virtually every market segment it serves.

Founded in 1994 through the merger of Swedish ASSA and Finnish Abloy, ASSA ABLOY has grown primarily through acquisition to become the dominant player in physical access control. The company has completed over 300 acquisitions, making it one of the most prolific serial acquirers in the building products industry. Its portfolio spans mechanical locks, electromechanical locks, digital access solutions, entrance automation, and security technology.

The company is well-positioned for the secular shift from mechanical to electronic and connected access solutions. The penetration of electronic locks remains low globally (estimated 5-10% of installed base), providing a long runway for growth as buildings upgrade to smart access control, mobile credentialing, and integrated security platforms.

## Key Financial Data

| Metric | FY 2025 | FY 2024 | Change |
|--------|---------|---------|--------|
| Revenue | SEK 152.4B | SEK 150.2B | +1.5% reported (+3% organic) |
| Full-Year Organic Growth | 3% | — | — |
| EBITDA Margin | 17.9% | — | Record |
| EBIT Margin | 16.8% | — | Record |
| Acquisitions Completed | 23 | — | — |

## Recent Earnings

### Q4 2025 Results (Reported February 5, 2026)

| Metric | Value | Change |
|--------|-------|--------|
| Organic Sales Growth | 4% | Accelerating |
| Full-Year Sales Growth | 1% (reported) | +3% organic |
| EBITDA Margin | 17.9% | Record |
| EBIT Margin | 16.8% | Record |

The Q4 results showed accelerating organic growth of 4% driven by strong demand for electronic access solutions and entrance automation. Record profitability margins reflect ongoing operational improvements and a favorable product mix shift toward higher-margin electronic and digital solutions. Shares rose 3.2% following the announcement.

## M&A Activity (2025-2026)

ASSA ABLOY completed 23 acquisitions in 2025 alone, maintaining its position as the most active acquirer in the access solutions industry. Key deals include:

| Date | Target | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| Early 2025 | Uhlmann & Zacher | German access control handles and knobs |
| Early 2025 | InVue | U.S. connected asset protection and access control |
| Spring 2025 | Pedestal PRO | U.S. access control pedestals and mounting solutions |
| Late Spring 2025 | TeleAlarm Group | German personal alarm and care technology |
| Summer 2025 | SiteOwl | Cloud-based physical security lifecycle management |
| 2025 | Calmell | Spanish smart cards and ticket manufacturer |
| 2025 | Door System | Danish fire-rated door manufacturer |
| Dec 2025 | Sargent & Greenleaf | Electronic and mechanical lock provider |
| Mar 2026 | Sennco Solutions | U.S. retail asset protection technology |

## Strategic Initiatives

- **Electrification of Access:** Accelerating the shift from mechanical to electronic access solutions, targeting significant growth in smart locks, mobile credentials, and cloud-based access management.
- **Acquisitive Growth Model:** Continuing the proven M&A engine with 20+ acquisitions annually, focusing on technology-led access solutions and geographic expansion.
- **Margin Expansion:** Record EBITDA and EBIT margins reflect ongoing operational efficiency improvements and mix enrichment.
- **Connected Solutions Platform:** Building an integrated digital ecosystem for physical security management.
- **Sustainability:** Increasing focus on sustainable manufacturing and product lifecycle environmental impact.

## Tariff & Trade Exposure

As a Swedish-headquartered company with global manufacturing operations, ASSA ABLOY faces FX translation risk (SEK vs. USD, EUR, GBP). Direct tariff exposure is moderated by local-for-local manufacturing in key markets. However, cross-border component flows (electronics, specialty metals) could be impacted by escalating trade tensions. The company's diversified geographic footprint provides natural hedging.

## 2026 Outlook & Guidance

| Metric | 2026 Target |
|--------|-------------|
| Pricing | 1.5-2% |
| Volume Growth | 2-3% |
| EBIT Margin Target | 16-17% range |

CEO Nico Delvaux has expressed confidence in navigating market conditions with continued strategic acquisition activity and strong organic growth momentum. The secular shift toward electronic access solutions provides structural tailwind independent of construction cycles.

## News Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| 2026-03 | Acquired Sennco Solutions (U.S. retail security) |
| 2026-02-05 | Q4 2025 results: 4% organic growth, record margins, shares +3.2% |
| 2025-12 | Acquired Sargent & Greenleaf |
| 2025 | Completed 23 acquisitions across access solutions categories |
| 2025 | Published 2025 Annual Report |

## Related Articles
- [Bain M&A Report](../../raw/articles/2026-04-06-bain-building-products-ma-report.md)

# Builders FirstSource Inc. (BLDR)

## Company Overview
Builders FirstSource is the largest U.S. supplier of structural building products, manufactured components, and construction services to professional homebuilders. The company operates approximately 570 locations across 43 states, serving residential new construction, repair and remodel, and light commercial end markets. Its product portfolio includes factory-built trusses and wall panels, windows, doors, lumber and lumber sheet goods, and millwork.

Following its 2021 merger with BMC Stock Holdings, Builders FirstSource has consolidated its position as the dominant player in value-added structural building products. The company's competitive moat lies in its vertically integrated manufacturing capabilities (trusses, panels, stairs), its local scale advantages, and its growing digital platform for homebuilder workflow solutions.

Despite its strong market position, BLDR has experienced two consecutive years of sales and earnings declines as housing starts remain below normalized levels due to affordability pressures and elevated mortgage rates. The company is now pivoting toward modular and offsite construction as a next growth vector.

## Key Financial Data

| Metric | FY 2025 | FY 2024 | Change |
|--------|---------|---------|--------|
| Net Sales | $15.19B | $16.4B | -7.4% |
| Core Organic Net Sales Change | — | — | -10.3% |
| Gross Profit Margin | ~30.5% | ~32.5% | -200 bps |
| Net Income | ~$660M | ~$1.63B | -59.6% |
| Free Cash Flow | $874M | — | — |

## Recent Earnings

### Q4 2025 Results (Reported February 17, 2026)

| Metric | Value | Change |
|--------|-------|--------|
| Net Sales | $3.36B | -12.1% YoY |
| EPS | $1.12 | Missed ($1.24 est.) |
| Gross Margin | 29.8% | -250 bps YoY |
| Adjusted EBITDA | $274.9M | -44.3% YoY |
| Adjusted EBITDA Margin | 8.2% | -470 bps YoY |

Revenue was below analyst estimates primarily driven by a below-normal starts environment. Gross profit fell to $1.0B from $1.2B in the prior year, reflecting both volume declines and commodity price deflation. Value-added products continued to outperform commodity products, though both categories saw declines.

### 2026 Guidance

| Metric | Range |
|--------|-------|
| Net Sales | $14.8B - $15.8B |
| Adjusted EBITDA | $1.3B - $1.7B |
| Adjusted EBITDA Margin | 8.8% - 10.8% |
| Free Cash Flow | ~$500M |

Management expects single-family and multifamily starts flat YoY with R&R up approximately 1%. The wide guidance range reflects significant macro uncertainty around housing starts, mortgage rates, and trade policy.

## M&A Activity (2025-2026)

Builders FirstSource has been an active acquirer since the BMC merger, completing 40+ acquisitions representing over $2.3B in annual revenue. M&A pace accelerated in recent years:

| Period | Acquisitions Completed |
|--------|----------------------|
| 2023 | 7 |
| 2024 | 8 |
| 2025 | 7 (including 4 in H2) |
| 2026 YTD | 1 (Pleasant Valley Homes) |

**Notable Recent Deals:**
- **Pleasant Valley Homes (February 2026):** Manufacturer of modular homes for builders and developers, expanding BLDR's prefabricated and factory-built housing capabilities.
- **Premium Building Components (January 2025):** Truss and component manufacturer.

Acquisitions completed in the trailing twelve months are projected to add approximately 1% to net sales growth. The company maintains an M&A war chest of $9-10B in total capital deployment capacity.

## Strategic Initiatives

- **Value-Added Mix Shift:** Continuing to grow higher-margin manufactured products (trusses, wall panels, stairs) as a percentage of total revenue.
- **Modular & Offsite Construction:** The Pleasant Valley Homes acquisition signals a push into factory-built housing to address labor shortages and affordability challenges.
- **Digital Platform:** Expanding digital tools for homebuilder workflow management, quoting, and design.
- **Capital Return:** Active share repurchase program alongside M&A spending.
- **Operational Efficiency:** Invested over $110M in new and expanded value-added operations to deepen local scale and improve product mix.

## Tariff & Trade Exposure

Builders FirstSource has meaningful exposure to lumber tariffs (Canadian softwood lumber duties) and potential tariffs on imported windows, doors, and hardware. Commodity lumber price volatility directly impacts revenue (though margins can benefit from deflation on existing inventory). The company's U.S.-focused manufacturing footprint provides some insulation, but imported components remain a cost factor.

## 2026 Outlook & Guidance

The 2026 outlook is cautious given the persistent housing affordability challenge. Key assumptions include:
- Single-family starts flat YoY (~1.0M)
- Multifamily starts flat YoY
- R&R market up ~1%
- Commodity prices relatively stable

Upside catalysts include any easing of mortgage rates, favorable housing policy, or acceleration in modular adoption. Downside risks center on further starts deterioration, tariff-driven cost inflation, or a broader economic slowdown.

## News Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| 2026-02-17 | Q4 2025 earnings: Revenue -12.1% YoY, missed estimates; 2026 guidance issued |
| 2026-02-00 | Acquired Pleasant Valley Homes (modular housing) |
| 2025-01-00 | Acquired Premium Building Components |
| 2025 | Completed 7 acquisitions throughout the year |

## Related Articles
- [Builders FirstSource Q4 2025 Earnings](../../raw/articles/2026-02-17-builders-firstsource-q4-earnings.md)
- [Q4 2025 Building Materials Earnings Preview](../../raw/articles/2026-01-20-q4-2025-building-materials-earnings-preview.md)
companies.title: Builders FirstSource Inc.

companies.content:
# Canfor Corporation (CFP.TO)

## Company Overview

Canfor Corporation is one of the world's largest producers of sustainable lumber and pulp products, headquartered in Vancouver, British Columbia. The company operates sawmills and pulp operations across western Canada, the US South, and Sweden. Canfor's product portfolio includes dimension lumber, specialty wood products, and pulp and paper through its subsidiary Canfor Pulp Products Inc.

Canfor has been undergoing a significant strategic transformation in response to persistent timber supply challenges in British Columbia, weak lumber demand, and escalating US trade duties. The company has closed multiple high-cost mills in Canada and the US Southeast, while shifting investment toward lower-cost operations in the US South and Sweden. Canfor completed the acquisition of the remaining minority stake in Canfor Pulp Products ahead of its expected TSX delisting in March 2026.

The company is one of the Canadian lumber producers most heavily impacted by US softwood lumber duties and the newer Section 232 tariffs, which together create substantial cost headwinds. Canfor recorded a $320.4 million asset write-down and impairment charge in Q4 2025, reflecting the severity of market conditions.

## Key Financial Data

| Metric | Q4 2025 | FY2025 | FY2024 |
|--------|---------|--------|--------|
| Operating Loss | $(415.9)M | - | - |
| Net Loss | $(390.5)M | - | - |
| EPS | $(3.35) | - | - |
| Adj. Operating Loss | $(145)M | - | $(82)M loss |
| Impairment Charges | $320.4M | - | - |
| 2026E Capital Spend (Lumber) | - | - | CAD 175M |
| 2026E Capital Spend (Pulp) | - | - | CAD 35M |

## Recent Earnings

**Q4 2025 Results:**
- Operating loss of $415.9 million and net loss of $390.5 million ($3.35/share)
- Adjusted operating loss of C$145 million versus C$82 million loss in prior-year quarter
- Asset write-down and impairment charge of approximately $320.4 million:
  - $213.9 million related to lumber segment
  - $106.5 million related to pulp and paper segment
  - Includes $52.5 million write-off of previously recognized deferred tax asset
- Results significantly missed analyst expectations; stock fell 6.11% post-earnings

**Key Drivers of Losses:**
- Persistently weak North American lumber demand
- Elevated softwood lumber duties (antidumping + countervailing duties exceeded 35%)
- Section 232 tariffs adding additional 10% on imports from Canada
- Timber supply challenges in northern British Columbia
- Low pulp prices impacting pulp segment

## M&A Activity (2025-2026)

| Date | Transaction | Details |
|------|------------|---------|
| 2025-2026 | Acquisition: Canfor Pulp minority stake | Acquired remaining minority shares; TSX delisting expected March 2026 |
| Aug 2025 | Divestiture: Estill sawmill (SC) | Permanent closure; 290 employees affected (combined with Darlington) |
| Aug 2025 | Divestiture: Darlington sawmill (SC) | Permanent closure; reduced US capacity by 350 MMBF annually |
| 2025 | Closure: Polar sawmill (Bear Lake, BC) | Permanent shutdown; 180 employees affected |
| 2025 | Closure: Various BC operations | Multiple curtailments and closures in northern BC |

## Strategic Initiatives

- **Geographic Shift:** Moving investment from high-cost British Columbia operations toward US South and Sweden
- **Mill Rationalization:** Permanent closure of uneconomic mills to reduce cost structure (Estill, Darlington, Polar, others)
- **Canfor Pulp Consolidation:** Acquired remaining minority stake; TSX delisting planned for March 2026
- **Capital Discipline:** Reduced 2026 capital spending plan to CAD 175M (lumber) + CAD 35M (pulp)
- **US South Investment:** Focus on lower-cost, duty-free lumber production in the US Southeast
- **Sweden Operations:** Maintaining and potentially expanding European lumber operations via Vida subsidiary

## Tariff & Trade Exposure

Canfor is among the most tariff-exposed lumber companies due to its heavy Canadian production base:
- **Softwood Lumber Duties:** Combined antidumping and countervailing duties on Canadian lumber shipments rose from 14.4% to over 35% in 2025
- **Section 232 Tariffs:** Additional 10% ad valorem duty on softwood timber and lumber imports effective October 14, 2025
- **Cumulative Impact:** Duties represent a massive cost burden on approximately 25% or more of Canfor's production
- **Mitigation Strategy:** Shift production to US South (duty-free) and Sweden (different trade relationship)
- **US South mills:** Provide natural hedge as domestic US production avoids import duties
- **Sweden operations:** Subject to different EU-US trade dynamics

## 2026 Outlook & Guidance

**Company Outlook:**
- North American lumber markets face continued uncertainty through 2026
- Volatility likely to persist amid challenging global economic conditions
- Unresolved trade issues and sustained higher duty rates create ongoing headwinds
- Some early signs of stabilization in certain US regions, but improvements remain fragile
- Overall market conditions projected to remain subdued through at least first half of 2026

**Capital Plans:**
- 2026 lumber capital spend: approximately CAD 175 million
- 2026 pulp capital spend: approximately CAD 35 million

**Key Risks:**
- Sustained high softwood lumber duty rates
- Section 232 tariff escalation
- Weak housing starts in US and Canada
- Timber supply constraints in British Columbia
- Low pulp prices
- Canadian dollar volatility

## News Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| Mar 2026 | Canfor Pulp Products TSX delisting expected |
| Mar 2026 | Q4 2025 results released; significant losses and impairments |
| Feb 2026 | $320.4M asset write-down and impairment charge announced |
| Oct 2025 | Section 232 tariffs on lumber imports take effect (10%) |
| Aug 2025 | Permanent closure of Estill and Darlington sawmills in South Carolina |
| Jun 2025 | Announced closure of Darlington and Estill sawmills |
| 2025 | Polar sawmill in Bear Lake permanently shut down |
| 2025 | Softwood lumber duties increased from 14.4% to over 35% |

## Related Articles

- Search knowledge base for articles mentioning "Canfor"
- Cross-reference with softwood lumber tariff coverage and BC forestry industry articles
companies.title: Canfor Corporation

# Carlisle Companies (CSL)

## Company Overview
Carlisle Companies is a leading diversified manufacturer of premium building products, primarily focused on commercial roofing, waterproofing, and insulation through its two main segments: Carlisle Construction Materials (CCM) and Carlisle Weatherproofing Technologies (CWT). The company serves the non-residential construction market with a strong emphasis on energy-efficient building envelope solutions.

Carlisle has undergone a significant portfolio transformation over the past several years, divesting non-core businesses (including its interconnect technologies segment) to focus purely on building products. The company's Vision 2030 strategy targets best-in-class EBITDA margins, return on invested capital, and disciplined capital allocation across M&A and shareholder returns.

With approximately $5.0B in annual revenue, Carlisle holds leading market positions in single-ply roofing membranes (TPO/EPDM), spray polyurethane foam insulation, and commercial waterproofing systems. The company benefits from structural tailwinds including the aging U.S. commercial roofing installed base, energy efficiency mandates, and growing demand for sustainable building materials.

## Key Financial Data

| Metric | FY 2025 | FY 2024 | Change |
|--------|---------|---------|--------|
| Revenue | $5.0B | ~$4.8B | +4% |
| Adjusted EPS | $19.40 | — | — |
| Adjusted EBITDA Margin | 24.4% | ~24% | +40 bps |
| ROIC | ~25% | — | — |
| Operating Cash Flow | $1.1B | — | — |
| Free Cash Flow | $972M | — | — |

## Recent Earnings

### Q4 2025 Results (Reported February 3, 2026)

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Revenue | $1.1B |
| Diluted EPS | $3.19 |
| Adjusted EPS | $3.90 |

Full-year results demonstrated Carlisle's premium margin profile and strong cash conversion. The company returned $1.5B to shareholders through $1.3B of share repurchases and $181M of dividends in 2025.

## M&A Activity (2025-2026)

Carlisle has a stated capital allocation target of deploying $4B into acquisitions between 2024-2026, though actual spending has lagged:

| Year | M&A Spending | Notable Deals |
|------|-------------|---------------|
| 2021 | ~$1.6B | Henry Company (largest deal to date) |
| 2025 | $110M | Bonded Logic / Phoenix Fibers |
| 2024-2026 Target | $4.0B | Pipeline remains active |

**Bonded Logic (May 2025):** Acquired select assets of Bonded Logic, Inc. and Phoenix Fibers, LLC, a U.S. manufacturer of sustainable thermal and acoustical insulation products with facilities in Arizona and Georgia. This acquisition expands Carlisle's sustainable insulation offerings.

The M&A environment in 2025 proved challenging (elevated valuations, limited quality targets), leading Carlisle to redirect capital toward share repurchases. Management expects to continue pursuing value-enhancing acquisitions in 2026.

## Strategic Initiatives

- **Vision 2030:** Framework targeting best-in-class margins, ROIC, and revenue growth through organic investment and M&A.
- **Sustainable Building Solutions:** Expanding energy-efficient roofing, insulation, and waterproofing product lines aligned with building codes and ESG mandates.
- **Pricing Discipline:** Maintaining price leadership in premium commercial roofing categories.
- **Capital Return:** Aggressive shareholder return program with plans to repurchase up to $1B of shares in 2026.
- **Acquisition Pipeline:** Targeting $4B deployment across 2024-2026 in building envelope adjacencies.

## Tariff & Trade Exposure

Carlisle's manufacturing footprint is predominantly U.S.-based, providing substantial insulation from tariff risk. However, certain raw materials (petrochemicals, specialty chemicals) may be subject to tariff-driven cost inflation. The company's pricing power and market-leading positions have historically enabled pass-through of input cost increases.

## 2026 Outlook & Guidance

| Metric | 2026 Guidance |
|--------|--------------|
| Revenue Growth | Low single digits (LSD) |
| EBITDA Margin Expansion | ~50 bps |
| Q1 2026 Revenue | Down LSD (seasonal) |
| Share Repurchases | Up to $1.0B |

Full-year revenue growth is expected to be driven by a stronger second half on seasonal and market improvements. The company's backlog and reroofing demand provide visibility into H2 acceleration.

## News Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| 2026-02-03 | Q4 2025 results: $5.0B full-year revenue, 24.4% adjusted EBITDA margin |
| 2025-05-00 | Acquired Bonded Logic / Phoenix Fibers (sustainable insulation) |
| 2025 | Published 2025 Annual Report showing 25% ROIC |

## Related Articles
- [Q4 2025 Building Materials Earnings Preview](../../raw/articles/2026-01-20-q4-2025-building-materials-earnings-preview.md)

# Carrier Global Corporation (CARR)

## Company Overview
Carrier Global Corporation is a global leader in intelligent climate and energy solutions, providing HVAC (heating, ventilation, and air conditioning), refrigeration, and fire & security products and services. The company was spun off from United Technologies in 2020 and has since undergone a dramatic portfolio transformation to become a pure-play climate and HVAC company.

The transformational acquisition of Viessmann Climate Solutions for EUR 12B (completed January 2024) made Carrier the leading provider of heating solutions in Europe, combining Viessmann's heat pump and boiler technology with Carrier's global HVAC platform. Simultaneously, Carrier divested its fire & security businesses and commercial refrigeration units to streamline its focus on climate solutions.

With approximately $21.75B in annual revenue, Carrier now operates primarily in commercial HVAC (including the growing data center cooling segment), residential HVAC and heating (strengthened by Viessmann), and transport refrigeration. The company is benefiting from strong secular demand for energy-efficient cooling, decarbonization solutions, and data center thermal management.

## Key Financial Data

| Metric | FY 2025 | FY 2024 | Change |
|--------|---------|---------|--------|
| Sales | $21.75B | — | — |
| Adjusted EPS | $2.59 | — | — |
| Free Cash Flow | $2.121B | — | — |
| Commercial HVAC Growth | Double-digit | — | 5th consecutive year |
| Aftermarket Growth | Double-digit | — | 5th consecutive year |

## Recent Earnings

### Q4 2025 Results

| Metric | Value | Change |
|--------|-------|--------|
| Sales | $4.8B | -6% YoY |
| Organic Sales | — | -9% YoY |
| EPS | $0.34 | Missed ($0.36 est.) |

Q4 results were weaker than expected, driven by organic sales decline of 9%. However, commercial HVAC orders were up nearly 50% in Q4, driven by major data center wins, suggesting strong momentum entering 2026. The aftermarket business continued its double-digit growth streak.

### Viessmann Integration Progress
The Viessmann acquisition has moved from being modestly dilutive to EPS in 2024 to accretive in 2025, with management targeting increasing accretion in 2026 and beyond. The European heat pump market has been recovering slowly but the long-term regulatory tailwind (EU Green Deal) remains intact.

## M&A Activity (2025-2026)

### Completed Transformational Deals
| Date | Transaction | Value | Description |
|------|------------|-------|-------------|
| Jan 2024 | Viessmann Climate Solutions (acquired) | EUR 12B | European heating, heat pumps, boilers |
| 2024-2025 | Fire & Security businesses (divested) | Multiple deals | Exited non-core segments |
| 2025 | Riello business (agreed to sell) | ~$430M | Continued portfolio simplification |
| 2024-2025 | Commercial refrigeration (divested) | — | Exited to focus on climate |

The portfolio transformation has turned Carrier into a focused climate and HVAC company, with ~100% of revenue from climate-related products and services.

## Strategic Initiatives

- **Data Center Cooling:** Targeting sixth consecutive year of double-digit commercial HVAC growth in 2026, driven by hyperscale and colocation data center builds. Q4 2025 orders up ~50%.
- **Viessmann Synergies:** Accelerating integration to capture procurement, manufacturing, and commercial synergies from the EUR 12B acquisition.
- **Decarbonization Solutions:** Positioning as a leader in the transition from fossil-fuel heating to heat pumps and electrified HVAC in Europe and North America.
- **Aftermarket & Services:** Building recurring revenue streams through service contracts, parts, and digital building management.
- **Capital Return:** Plans to repurchase $1.5B in shares during 2026.
- **Portfolio Simplification:** Completing remaining divestitures (Riello) to achieve pure-play climate positioning.

## Tariff & Trade Exposure

Carrier faces moderate tariff exposure across its global operations:
- European manufacturing (Viessmann) could be impacted by U.S.-EU trade tensions
- Chinese HVAC operations and component sourcing exposed to U.S.-China tariffs
- Refrigerant supply chains may be affected by trade policy on specialty chemicals
- The company's global manufacturing footprint (North America, Europe, Asia) provides some production flexibility

## 2026 Outlook & Guidance

| Metric | 2026 Guidance |
|--------|--------------|
| Reported Sales | ~$22B |
| Organic Growth | Flat to low single digits |
| Adjusted EPS | ~$2.80 |
| Free Cash Flow | ~$2.0B |
| Share Repurchases | $1.5B |

The guidance implies solid earnings growth driven by commercial HVAC momentum (especially data centers), continued Viessmann accretion, and share buybacks. The cautious organic growth outlook reflects uncertainty in residential markets and European economic conditions.

## News Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| 2026-02 | Q4/FY 2025 results: $21.75B revenue, $2.59 adj. EPS; 2026 outlook of $2.80 adj. EPS |
| 2025-Q4 | Commercial HVAC orders up ~50%, led by data center wins |
| 2025 | Agreed to sell Riello business for ~$430M |
| 2025 | Completed divestitures of fire, security, and commercial refrigeration units |
| 2024-01 | Completed EUR 12B Viessmann Climate Solutions acquisition |

## Related Articles
- [Q4 2025 Building Materials Earnings Preview](../../raw/articles/2026-01-20-q4-2025-building-materials-earnings-preview.md)

# CEMEX (CX)

## Company Overview

CEMEX is a global building materials company headquartered in Monterrey, Mexico, and one of the world's largest producers of cement, ready-mix concrete, and aggregates. The company operates in more than 50 countries across the Americas, Europe, Africa, the Middle East, and Asia, with a particularly strong presence in Mexico, the United States, and select European markets (UK, Germany, Poland, Czech Republic, Spain).

CEMEX has undergone a significant strategic transformation over the past several years, shifting from a heavily leveraged, commodity-focused cement producer to a more diversified building materials platform with an emphasis on higher-margin performance products, urbanization solutions, and sustainability. The company's "Sprint" strategic framework (2026-2027) targets accelerated EBITDA growth and margin expansion while maintaining disciplined capital allocation.

With trailing twelve-month revenue of approximately $15.8 billion and a growing presence in the U.S. market through bolt-on acquisitions, CEMEX competes with global peers like Holcim, Heidelberg Materials, and CRH while maintaining a differentiated position through its Mexican production base and cross-border logistics capabilities.

## Key Financial Data

| Metric | FY 2025E | FY 2024 | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| Revenue (TTM as of Sep 2025) | ~$15.8B | $16.2B | ~-2% |
| Q3 2025 Revenue | $4.25B | -- | +4.7% QoQ |
| Q2 2025 Net Sales | $4.1B | -- | -- |
| Q2 2025 EBITDA | $823M | -- | -- |
| Q3 2025 EBITDA Growth | +19% YoY | -- | -- |
| H1 2025 Net Income | Record | -- | -- |

## Recent Earnings

**Q3 2025 Results:**

CEMEX reported revenue of $4.25 billion for the quarter ending September 30, 2025, representing 4.7% growth. The standout metric was EBITDA growth of 19% year-over-year, demonstrating the company's margin expansion strategy is gaining traction. Double-digit EBITDA growth and margin expansion was achieved across all geographic regions.

**H1 2025 Results:**

CEMEX accelerated its strategic transformation and reported record net income in the first half of 2025. Q2 2025 net sales were $4.1 billion with EBITDA of $823 million.

**Revenue Trend:**

Full-year 2024 revenue was $16.2 billion, down 2.1% year-over-year. The trailing twelve-month revenue through September 2025 showed a similar trend at approximately $15.8 billion, reflecting volume softness in some markets offset by pricing gains and mix improvement.

## M&A Activity (2025-2026)

**Omega Products International (announced February 2026):**

CEMEX reached an agreement to acquire Omega Products International, a leading stucco manufacturer in the western United States with approximately $23 million in annual EBITDA. Omega operates four production facilities in California, Nevada, and Colorado, with over 50 years of experience. The transaction is expected to close in Q1 2026. This deal aligns with CEMEX's strategy to expand into higher-margin performance materials in the U.S.

**Organizational M&A Build-out:**

In November 2025, CEMEX US appointed Will Price as Senior Vice President of Mergers & Acquisitions to lead strategic growth initiatives through targeted acquisitions, divestitures, and corporate development activities across the U.S. business. This signals an accelerating M&A pipeline.

**Strategic Focus:**

CEMEX's bolt-on M&A focus remains primarily in the U.S. in highly synergistic businesses such as aggregates and mortars, while selectively divesting non-core assets globally.

## Strategic Initiatives

- **Sprint Framework (2026-2027):** CEMEX expects sales and EBITDA to grow annually by approximately 5% and 10%, respectively. Targets 45% free cash flow conversion in 2026 and 50% by 2027.
- **Performance Materials:** Expanding beyond commodity cement into stucco, mortars, and specialty products with higher margins and less cyclicality.
- **Mexico demand growth:** Expects 2.5-3% demand growth in Mexico in 2026, supported by nearshoring trends and infrastructure investment.
- **European pricing:** Plans potential price increases in Europe and the U.S. to offset inflation.
- **Sustainability:** CEMEX has been an early mover in alternative fuels and clinker substitution, with a growing portfolio of low-carbon products.

## Tariff & Trade Exposure

CEMEX has significant and complex tariff exposure given its Mexico-based production and cross-border trade:

- **25% U.S. tariff on Mexican cement (effective April 5, 2025):** The U.S. imposed a 25% tariff on cement imports from Canada and Mexico. However, CEMEX products compliant with USMCA rules of origin may be exempt. CEMEX has stated it is in a "good position" to manage tariff impacts.
- **Surcharge pass-through:** CEMEX announced it would introduce an immediate surcharge to pass tariff costs to consumers.
- **Cross-border supply optimization:** Plans to supply more from Mexican plants via rail (Torreon plant in central Mexico) and sea (east Mexican coastal plants) to optimize logistics costs against tariff impacts.
- **Historic antidumping context:** The U.S. and Mexico agreed to resolve a longstanding antidumping order on Mexican cement, which had been in place for decades. The tariff environment has effectively replaced this with a broader trade barrier.
- **Net beneficiary potential:** If tariffs reduce cement import competition in the U.S. market, CEMEX's domestic U.S. production capacity could see improved utilization and pricing power.

## 2026 Outlook & Guidance

**Sprint Framework Targets (2026-2027):**
- Sales growth: ~5% annually
- EBITDA growth: ~10% annually
- Free cash flow conversion: 45% in 2026, 50% by 2027
- Mexico demand growth: 2.5-3% in 2026
- Continued focus on deleveraging and investment-grade credit metrics

**Key Catalysts:**
- U.S. infrastructure spending ramp under IIJA
- Nearshoring-driven industrial construction in Mexico
- Margin expansion from operational efficiency and pricing
- Bolt-on M&A in higher-margin U.S. segments
- Potential resolution or exemption from tariff headwinds

## News Timeline

| Date | Event |
|---|---|
| Feb 26, 2026 | Announces acquisition of Omega Products International (stucco, western U.S.) |
| Feb 2026 | Q4 2025 / FY 2025 earnings release expected |
| Nov 2025 | Appoints Will Price as SVP of M&A for U.S. business |
| Q3 2025 | Reports 19% EBITDA growth, revenue of $4.25B |
| H1 2025 | Record net income in first half, net sales $4.1B in Q2 |
| Apr 2025 | 25% U.S. tariff on Mexican cement takes effect |

## Related Articles

- See knowledge-base raw articles on Mexico cement tariffs, U.S. building materials sector, and nearshoring construction demand.

companies.content:
# CRH plc (CRH)

## Company Overview

CRH plc is the largest building materials company in North America and among the largest globally, with operations spanning 28 countries. Headquartered in Dublin, Ireland, and listed on the NYSE following its 2023 primary listing move from the London Stock Exchange, CRH produces and distributes cement, aggregates, ready-mixed concrete, asphalt, and a broad range of building products. The company was added to the S&P 500 index in 2023, marking a transformative milestone in its capital markets profile.

CRH operates through two primary segments: Americas Materials Solutions and Building Solutions, and International Materials Solutions and Products. The Americas segment is the dominant earnings driver, benefiting from robust U.S. infrastructure spending underpinned by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA) and state-level highway programs. Internationally, CRH maintains significant positions in Europe, including cement and aggregates operations across the UK, Ireland, continental Europe, and emerging markets.

With a market capitalization exceeding $60 billion, CRH is the clear scale leader in North American heavy-side building materials. Its vertically integrated model -- controlling quarries, cement plants, ready-mix operations, and asphalt paving -- provides significant competitive moats through logistics advantages and permitting barriers. The company's disciplined M&A program has been a hallmark of its growth strategy for decades.

## Key Financial Data

| Metric | FY 2025 | FY 2024 | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| Total Revenue | $37.4B | $35.6B | +5% |
| Adjusted EBITDA | $7.7B | $6.9B | +11% |
| Net Income | $3.8B | $3.5B | +8% |
| Adjusted EBITDA Margin | 20.5% | 19.5% | +100 bps |
| Net Income Margin | 10.1% | 9.9% | +20 bps |
| Capital Deployed in M&A | $5.8B | -- | -- |
| Acquisitions Completed | 38 | -- | -- |

## Recent Earnings

**Q4 and Full-Year 2025 (reported February 18, 2026):**

CRH delivered record full-year results in 2025. Total revenues of $37.4 billion were 5% ahead of the prior year, driven by favorable end-market demand, disciplined commercial execution, and contributions from acquisitions. Adjusted EBITDA of $7.7 billion increased 11%, reflecting continued pricing momentum, acquisition contributions, and disciplined cost control. Net income of $3.8 billion was 8% ahead of the prior year.

Both net income margin (10.1%) and Adjusted EBITDA margin (20.5%) expanded year-over-year, demonstrating operational leverage and the accretive nature of recent acquisitions. The stock declined modestly on a slight EPS miss relative to consensus despite otherwise strong results.

## M&A Activity (2025-2026)

CRH deployed $5.8 billion in value-accretive growth investments during 2025, completing 38 acquisitions.

**Key Transactions:**

- **Eco Material Technologies ($2.1B, completed September 2025):** North America's leading supplier of Supplementary Cementitious Materials (SCMs), including fly ash and natural pozzolans. This positions CRH to capitalize on the growing demand for low-carbon concrete.
- **North American Aggregates (December 2025):** Aggregates assets acquisition expanding CRH's footprint; sold Minnesota aggregates and FOB asphalt assets to Martin Marietta.
- **27+ bolt-on acquisitions through Q3 2025:** Smaller deals across cement, aggregates, and building products in key U.S. and European markets.

**2026 M&A Outlook:**

At its September 2025 Investor Day, CRH outlined $40 billion of financial capacity over 2026-2030 (after maintenance capex), signaling aggressive acquisition ambitions will continue. Management has historically averaged $3-5 billion in annual M&A spending and is expected to maintain or accelerate this pace.

## Strategic Initiatives

- **2030 Financial Targets (announced September 2025 Investor Day):** 7-9% annual revenue growth (organic + inorganic), Adjusted EBITDA margins of 22-24%, and adjusted free cash flow conversion exceeding 100%.
- **S&P 500 membership:** Continues to drive index fund inflows and broaden the U.S. investor base.
- **Infrastructure positioning:** CRH is the single largest beneficiary of U.S. federal and state infrastructure spending among publicly traded building materials companies, with leading positions in highway paving, bridges, and utility construction.
- **Sustainability:** Eco Material Technologies acquisition positions CRH as a leader in supplementary cementitious materials, supporting the industry transition to lower-carbon concrete.
- **Vertical integration:** Continued emphasis on controlling the full value chain from quarry to final placement, creating logistics moats and pricing power.

## Tariff & Trade Exposure

CRH is among the least exposed building materials companies to tariff risk, as the vast majority of its products (aggregates, cement, ready-mix concrete, asphalt) are produced and consumed domestically. Key considerations:

- **Minimal import exposure:** Aggregates and cement are heavy, low-value-per-ton commodities that are overwhelmingly sourced locally. CRH's U.S. operations are primarily supplied from domestic quarries and cement plants.
- **Indirect benefit:** Tariffs on imported steel (50%) and aluminum (50%) increase costs for competing building materials (steel structures), making concrete and asphalt-based solutions relatively more attractive.
- **Input cost exposure:** Some equipment, spare parts, and specialty materials may face modest tariff impacts, but these are a small share of total costs.
- **Infrastructure spending risk:** The IIJA is scheduled to expire in October 2026, and broader federal spending cuts could slow future project flow, which would be a larger risk than tariffs for CRH.

## 2026 Outlook & Guidance

**Company Guidance (FY 2026):**
- Net Income: $3.9B - $4.1B (approximately +3-8% YoY)
- Adjusted EBITDA: $8.1B - $8.5B (approximately +5-10% YoY)

Management expects further growth driven by continued U.S. infrastructure demand, pricing momentum, and ongoing acquisition contributions. The demand backdrop remains favorable across public construction (highway, utility, water) and is expected to improve in private nonresidential segments.

**Key Catalysts:**
- IIJA project lettings continuing to ramp through 2026
- State-level transportation budgets at or near record levels
- Continued M&A deployment at scale
- Margin expansion toward 22-24% EBITDA margin target by 2030

## News Timeline

| Date | Event |
|---|---|
| Feb 18, 2026 | Reports record FY 2025 results: $37.4B revenue, $7.7B EBITDA |
| Dec 2025 | Completes North American aggregates acquisition; sells Minnesota assets to Martin Marietta |
| Sep 30, 2025 | Investor Day: announces 2030 targets, $40B financial capacity |
| Sep 2025 | Completes $2.1B Eco Material Technologies acquisition |
| Nov 2025 | Reports Q3 2025 results showing continued momentum |
| 2025 | Completes 38 total acquisitions deploying $5.8B |

## Related Articles

- See knowledge-base raw articles mentioning CRH, building materials sector coverage, and infrastructure spending analysis.
companies.title: CRH plc

companies.content:
# Daikin Industries (6367.T)

## Company Overview
Daikin Industries, Ltd. is the world's largest manufacturer of HVAC (heating, ventilation, and air conditioning) systems, headquartered in Osaka, Japan. With a fiscal year ending March 31, the company operates across three core segments: HVAC & Refrigeration (air conditioners, heat pumps, applied systems), Chemicals (fluorochemicals, refrigerants), and Other (oil hydraulics, defense). Daikin is the global leader in both residential and commercial air conditioning, holding number one or two market positions across Japan, Asia, Europe, and North America.

Daikin's HVAC dominance spans the full spectrum from residential split systems to large-scale applied commercial systems, including the rapidly growing data center cooling segment. The company's Goodman brand (acquired in 2012) is a leading North American residential HVAC manufacturer, while Daikin Applied provides commercial and industrial HVAC solutions. The Chemicals segment produces fluoropolymers and refrigerants, positioning Daikin uniquely across both the equipment and consumable sides of the climate control industry.

The company has been rapidly expanding its data center cooling capabilities through targeted acquisitions and new manufacturing investments, recognizing the AI-driven explosion in cooling demand for hyperscale facilities.

## Key Financial Data

| Metric | FY2025 (Mar 2025) | FY2024 | Change |
|--------|-------------------|---------|--------|
| Net Sales | JPY 4,752.3B (~$32B) | JPY 4,396.0B | +8.1% |
| Operating Profit | JPY 401.7B | JPY 392.3B | +2.4% |

| Metric | 9M FY2026 (Apr-Dec 2025) | 9M FY2025 | Change |
|--------|--------------------------|-----------|--------|
| Revenue | JPY 3,666B | JPY 3,594B | +2.0% |
| Operating Profit | JPY 307.9B | JPY 318.7B | -3.4% |
| Net Profit (to owners) | JPY 195.4B | JPY 186.8B | +4.6% |
| EPS | JPY 667.22 | JPY 637.72 | +4.6% |

## Recent Earnings

### Q3 FY2026 (Nine Months Ended December 31, 2025)
Revenue grew 2.0% to JPY 3.666 trillion but operating profit declined 3.4% to JPY 307.9B, reflecting cost pressures and margin compression in certain geographies. Net profit rose 4.6% to JPY 195.4B due to below-the-line items.

Daikin raised its full-year sales forecast to JPY 4.920T (up 1.7% from prior guidance) but lowered its operating profit forecast to JPY 413.0B (down 5.1%), reflecting the challenging operating environment including U.S. tariff uncertainty and input cost pressures.

## M&A Activity (2025-2026)

Daikin has made targeted data center cooling acquisitions in 2025-2026:

| Date | Target | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| Aug 2025 | DDC Solutions | San Diego-based ultra-high-density cooling cabinets and management software for data centers |
| Nov 2025 | Chilldyne | Negative pressure liquid cooling systems for high-performance and AI data centers |
| Dec 2025 | Anh Nguyen Trading Technical Service | Vietnamese HVAC/BMS integration company (via Daikin Air Conditioning Vietnam) |

**Capacity Expansion:**
- New modular solutions manufacturing facility opening January 2026 to meet data center demand
- Two new manufacturing facilities being built to expand production capacity

## Strategic Initiatives

- **Data Center Cooling Leadership:** Building comprehensive end-to-end cooling solutions for AI and hyperscale data centers through acquisitions (DDC Solutions, Chilldyne) and new manufacturing capacity.
- **Global HVAC Leadership:** Maintaining world number one position across residential and commercial HVAC categories.
- **Heat Pump Expansion:** Growing European heat pump presence aligned with EU decarbonization mandates and the transition from fossil heating.
- **Chemical Synergies:** Leveraging fluorochemical expertise in next-generation low-GWP refrigerants required by regulatory phase-downs.
- **Emerging Market Growth:** Expanding market share in Southeast Asia, India, and other high-growth markets.
- **Manufacturing Investment:** Significant capacity additions across multiple geographies to meet growing demand.

## Tariff & Trade Exposure

Daikin faces multiple tariff and trade headwinds:
- U.S. tariff policies creating uncertainty for Goodman's North American operations (some components imported from Asia)
- JPY weakness/volatility impacting reported results and cross-border manufacturing economics
- European regulatory requirements for refrigerant transitions adding compliance costs
- China manufacturing operations exposed to potential trade restrictions
- Global supply chain disruptions for electronic components and specialty metals

Management has highlighted U.S. tariff policies as a key factor weighing on the global economy during Q3 results.

## 2026 Outlook & Guidance

### FY2026 Revised Forecast (Year Ending March 31, 2026)

| Metric | Forecast | Vs. Prior |
|--------|----------|-----------|
| Net Sales | JPY 4,920B (~$33B) | +1.7% revision |
| Operating Profit | JPY 413.0B | -5.1% revision |

The divergence between upwardly revised sales and downwardly revised operating profit reflects cost pressures, tariff uncertainty, and investment in growth initiatives (particularly data center cooling capacity). The company is prioritizing market share and capability building over near-term margin optimization.

## News Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| 2026-02-04 | Q3 FY2026 results: Revenue +2.0%, OP -3.4%; revised FY guidance |
| 2026-01 | New modular solutions manufacturing facility opened for data center cooling |
| 2025-12 | Signed agreement to acquire Anh Nguyen in Vietnam |
| 2025-11 | Acquired Chilldyne (liquid cooling for AI data centers) |
| 2025-08 | Acquired DDC Solutions (high-density data center cooling) |
| 2025-05 | FY2025 results: JPY 4,752B revenue (+8.1%) |

# Fortune Brands Innovations (FBIN)

## Company Overview
Fortune Brands Innovations is a home and security products company operating through three segments: Water Innovations (Moen faucets and fixtures), Outdoors & Security (Therma-Tru doors, Fiberon decking, Master Lock, SentrySafe), and Connected Products (Yale and August smart locks, digital security). Headquartered in Deerfield, Illinois, the company was formed from the 2022 transformation of Fortune Brands Home & Security into a digitally-focused innovator.

Fortune Brands holds the number one or two market share position in most of its core categories, with Moen being the top-selling faucet brand in North America. The company has strategically pivoted toward connected and smart home products, highlighted by the $800M acquisition of Assa Abloy's residential business (Yale and August smart locks) in 2023, which created the Connected Products segment.

With approximately $4.5B in annual revenue, Fortune Brands is positioned at the intersection of plumbing, security, and smart home technology. The company generates the majority of its revenue from repair and remodel activity, providing some insulation from new construction cyclicality.

## Key Financial Data

| Metric | FY 2025 | FY 2024 | Change |
|--------|---------|---------|--------|
| Sales | $4.5B | $4.6B | -3% (-1% ex-China) |
| EPS (GAAP) | $2.47 | $3.74 | -34% |
| EPS (Before Charges/Gains) | $3.61 | $4.10 | -12% |
| Operating Cash Flow | $478.6M | — | — |
| Free Cash Flow | $366.8M | — | — |
| Cash Conversion Ratio | 122.8% | — | — |
| Net Debt | $2.3B | — | 2.6x EBITDA |

## Recent Earnings

### Q4 2025 Results (Reported February 5, 2026)

| Metric | Value | Change |
|--------|-------|--------|
| Sales | $1.1B | -2% YoY |
| EPS (GAAP) | $0.63 | -25% YoY |
| EPS (Before Charges/Gains) | $0.86 | -12% YoY |

Results reflected continued softness in China (impacting Water Innovations segment) and slower discretionary renovation spending in North America. The company noted outperformance relative to end-market conditions, suggesting market share gains in core categories.

## M&A Activity (2025-2026)

| Date | Transaction | Description |
|------|------------|-------------|
| 2024-02 | SpringWell Water Filtration | Acquired residential whole-home water filtration and softening (D2C channel) |
| 2024-06 | Value Hybrid (minority stake) | Strategic partnership for connected lockout tagout (cLOTO) solutions |
| 2023 | Yale & August (from Assa Abloy) | ~$800M; residential smart locks and digital access |

No major acquisitions in 2025; the company focused on deleveraging and integrating prior deals. A new $1.25B credit facility secured in January 2026 provides financial flexibility for future acquisitions.

## Strategic Initiatives

- **Digital & Connected Transformation:** Building a connected home ecosystem through Yale/August smart locks, digital water products (Moen Smart Water), and integrated security solutions.
- **China Restructuring:** Addressing persistent weakness in the Chinese market, which has been a drag on Water Innovations segment results.
- **CEO Transition:** Board appointed Amit Banati as CEO effective May 13, 2026, succeeding Nicholas Fink (who will resign as CEO and director on April 1, 2026). Banati brings experience from Kellogg/Kellanova.
- **Water Innovation:** Expanding Moen into whole-home water management with filtration, leak detection, and smart water monitoring products.
- **Margin Recovery:** Pursuing cost reduction and productivity programs to restore margins toward historical levels.

## Tariff & Trade Exposure

Fortune Brands has meaningful tariff exposure, particularly in its Water Innovations segment:
- Significant sourcing from China for Moen products and components, directly exposed to U.S.-China tariffs
- Chinese market weakness has been exacerbated by trade tensions
- Some Master Lock and security product manufacturing in Asia
- The company has been actively reshoring and diversifying its supply chain to mitigate tariff impacts

Management has noted tariff-related cost headwinds as a factor in 2026 guidance.

## 2026 Outlook & Guidance

| Metric | 2026 Guidance |
|--------|--------------|
| Net Sales Growth | Flat to +2.0% |
| EPS (Before Charges/Gains) | $3.35 - $3.65 |
| Operating Margin (Before Charges/Gains) | 14.5% - 15.5% |
| Free Cash Flow | $400M - $450M |

The guidance assumes a modest improvement in North American R&R demand and continued weakness in China. The CEO transition introduces additional execution uncertainty. Key catalysts include connected product adoption acceleration, China stabilization, and eventual housing market recovery.

## News Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| 2026-02-05 | Q4/FY 2025 results: Sales -3%, EPS -12% adj.; CEO succession announced |
| 2026-01-16 | New $1.25B revolving credit facility secured |
| 2026 | Amit Banati appointed as incoming CEO (effective May 13, 2026) |
| 2025 | Nicholas Fink to resign as CEO on April 1, 2026 |

## Related Articles

# Geberit AG (GEBN.SW)

## Company Overview
Geberit is the European market leader in sanitary products and piping systems, headquartered in Rapperswil-Jona, Switzerland. The company operates across three product areas: Installation & Flushing Systems (concealed cisterns, actuator plates, flushing systems), Piping Systems (supply and drainage piping, fittings), and Bathroom Systems (bathroom ceramics, furniture, shower products).

Founded in 1874, Geberit has built a dominant position in European sanitary technology through a combination of product innovation, plumber-focused go-to-market strategy, and premium brand positioning. The company sells primarily through professional plumbing trade channels and has developed deep relationships with installers, architects, and specifiers. Geberit's products are specified in new construction and renovation projects across residential and commercial buildings.

With CHF 3.16B in annual revenue and EBITDA margins near 30%, Geberit is one of the most profitable companies in the building products sector. The company generates approximately 95% of revenue in Europe, making it heavily dependent on European construction activity. Germany is the single largest market, followed by Switzerland, Benelux, Nordics, and Southern Europe.

## Key Financial Data

| Metric | FY 2025 | FY 2024 | Change |
|--------|---------|---------|--------|
| Net Sales | CHF 3.16B | CHF 3.08B | +2.5% (CHF), +4.8% (local currency) |
| Net Income | CHF 598M | CHF 597M | +0.1% |
| EBITDA | CHF 931M | CHF 913M | +2.0% |
| EBITDA Margin | 29.4% | 29.6% | -20 bps |
| EPS | CHF 18.15 | CHF 18.06 | +0.5% |
| Net Debt / EBITDA | 1.2x | — | — |

## Recent Earnings

### Full Year 2025 Results

Net sales grew 4.8% in local currencies driven by volume recovery, though CHF-reported growth was dampened to 2.5% by currency headwinds. The volume growth marked a return to positive territory after two years of declining volumes in European construction markets.

**Regional Performance:**
- **Germany:** +5.6% (currency-adjusted) - strongest performance in the core market
- **Middle East & Africa:** +24.8% (currency-adjusted) - fastest growth region
- **Far East & Pacific:** -0.6% - Chinese market continued to decline

Net income was essentially flat at CHF 598M as EBITDA margin slipped 20 bps to 29.4%, reflecting higher input costs and investment in new marketing initiatives. EPS rose 0.5% to CHF 18.15, outpacing net income growth due to share buybacks.

## M&A Activity (2025-2026)

Geberit has pursued a highly disciplined approach to M&A, with no significant acquisitions announced in 2025-2026. The company prefers organic growth through innovation and market penetration over acquisitive expansion.

**Financial Capacity:** Net debt to EBITDA of 1.2x provides significant balance sheet flexibility for potential tuck-in acquisitions, but management has indicated strict return criteria must be met.

**Share Buyback Program (2024-2026):** A CHF 300M share repurchase program launched in September 2024 is ongoing, representing the primary capital return mechanism alongside dividends.

## Strategic Initiatives

- **New Marketing Initiative:** Launching a dedicated consumer/architect marketing program with incremental spending of CHF 20M in 2026 to increase brand pull-through with end consumers and designers.
- **Product Innovation:** Continuous new product introductions across all three product areas, including smart bathroom solutions and water-efficient technologies.
- **Digitalization & AI:** Ongoing investment in digital tools for plumber training, specification support, and AI-enhanced product development.
- **Sustainability Leadership:** Market-leading position in water-efficient products supporting European Green Deal and building renovation mandates.
- **Selective M&A:** Open to bolt-on acquisitions meeting strict criteria, with balance sheet capacity to support deals.

## Tariff & Trade Exposure

Geberit's manufacturing footprint is predominantly European (plants in Germany, Switzerland, Austria, Poland, Ukraine, and elsewhere in Europe), with products sold primarily within Europe. This local-for-local model limits direct tariff exposure. However:
- Raw material costs (brass, copper, ceramics, plastics) can be affected by global trade dynamics
- CHF strength against EUR remains a persistent headwind given the company reports in CHF but generates most revenue in EUR
- Minimal direct U.S./China tariff exposure given European market focus

## 2026 Outlook & Guidance

Geberit has indicated that "slight market growth is expected in 2026 overall, but no market recovery yet." The company expects:
- Slightly higher direct material prices in Q1 2026 vs. Q4 2025 (though down YoY)
- Incremental CHF 20M marketing investment
- Continued share buyback program execution

The next earnings report is scheduled for May 5, 2026. Key catalysts include European renovation activity (driven by energy efficiency mandates), German construction market stabilization, and continued Middle East growth.

## News Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| 2026-03 | Published 2025 Annual Report |
| 2026-02 | FY 2025 results: CHF 3.16B revenue (+4.8% local currency), flat net income |
| 2025-09 | Continued share buyback program (CHF 300M, 2024-2026) |

## Related Articles
- [Bain M&A Report](../../raw/articles/2026-04-06-bain-building-products-ma-report.md)
